mission.title: crossplane: compositionRevisionRef needs to be consistent when managing multiple clusters
mission.description: compositionRevisionRef needs to be consistent when managing multiple clusters. Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current crossplane deployment
steps.description:
Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What problem are you facing?

Although this feature is not yet released (slated for 1.4), we have identified a necessary area of improvement for those who manage many kubernetes clusters.

steps.title: Apply the fix for compositionRevisionRef needs to be consistent when managing…
steps.description:
### Description of your changes

In this PR, we're implementing Composition Revision Selectors and changing revision creation/selection logics to utilize them.
- While calculating the composition hash, we start to include composition labels and annotations. This means, we create a new revision each
```yaml
Every 2.0s: kubectl get compositionrevisions -o=custom-columns=NAME:...  ezgis-mbp: Mon Nov 14 15:28:59 2022

NAME                                              REVISION   LABEL
ezginetworks.aws.platformref.upbound.io-1f491cf   1          dev
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "compositionRevisionRef needs to be consistent when managing…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Description of your changes

In this PR, we're implementing Composition Revision Selectors and changing revision creation/selection logics to utilize them.
- While calculating the composition hash, we start to include composition labels and annotations.
codeSnippets:
Every 2.0s: kubectl get compositionrevisions -o=custom-columns=NAME:...  ezgis-mbp: Mon Nov 14 15:28:59 2022

NAME                                              REVISION   LABEL
ezginetworks.aws.platformref.upbound.io-1f491cf   1          dev

Every 2.0s: kubectl get claim -o=custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,...  ezgis-mbp: Mon Nov 14 15:29:30 2022

NAME          SYNCED   REVISION                                          POLICY      MATCHLABEL
vpc-auto      True     ezginetworks.aws.platformref.upbound.io-1f491cf   Automatic   <none>
vpc-dev       True     ezginetworks.aws.platformref.upbound.io-1f491cf   Automatic   map[env:dev]
vpc-dev-man   True     ezginetworks.aws.platformref.upbound.io-1f491cf   Manual      <none>
vpc-staging   True     <none>                                            Automatic   map[env:staging]

Every 2.0s: kubectl get composite -o=custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.n...  ezgis-mbp: Mon Nov 14 15:29:48 2022

NAME                REVISION                                          POLICY      ID
vpc-auto-qfrdl      ezginetworks.aws.platformref.upbound.io-1f491cf   Automatic   vpc-auto
vpc-dev-9dhvd       ezginetworks.aws.platformref.upbound.io-1f491cf   Automatic   vpc-dev
vpc-dev-man-jvklt   ezginetworks.aws.platformref.upbound.io-1f491cf   Manual      vpc-dev-man
vpc-staging-ww4fs   <none>                                            Automatic   vpc-staging

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
